---
title: "Mercado Laboral"
subtitle: "Empleo y desempleo en Chile"
date: last-modified
---

```{python}
#| label: setup
#| include: false

import pandas as pd
import plotly.express as px
import plotly.graph_objects as go

url = "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Economia-y-politicas-publicas/chiledatos/main/data/trabajo.csv"

dt = pd.read_csv(url, sep=",", encoding="utf-8")
dt = dt.rename(columns={
    "Tiempo": "periodo", "Año": "año", "Mes": "mes",
    "Total": "total", "Hombre": "hombre", "Mujer": "mujer"
})
dt[["año","mes","total","hombre","mujer"]] = dt[["año","mes","total","hombre","mujer"]].apply(pd.to_numeric, errors="coerce")
dt = dt.dropna(subset=["año","mes","total"])
dt = dt[(dt["año"] >= 2010) & (dt["año"] <= 2030)]
dt["fecha"] = pd.to_datetime(dict(year=dt["año"], month=dt["mes"], day=1))
dt = dt.sort_values("fecha").reset_index(drop=True)
dt["brecha"] = dt["mujer"] - dt["hombre"]

AUTOR = "Autor: Juan Miguieles Onetto | Fuente: INE"
NOTA = "Nota: La tasa de desempleo se mide trimestralmente mediante la ENE."

def agregar_autor(fig):
    fig.update_layout(
        annotations=[
            dict(text=AUTOR, x=0, y=-0.18, xref="paper", yref="paper",
                 showarrow=False, font=dict(size=10, color="gray"), xanchor="left"),
            dict(text=NOTA, x=0, y=-0.24, xref="paper", yref="paper",
                 showarrow=False, font=dict(size=9, color="#aaaaaa"), xanchor="left"),
        ]
    )
    return fig

eje_x = dict(
    dtick="M3",
    tickformat="%b %Y",
    tickangle=45,
    tickfont=dict(size=10),
)
```

## 1. Tasa de desempleo total

```{python}
fig = px.line(
    dt, x="fecha", y="total",
    labels={"total": "Tasa de desempleo (%)", "fecha": ""},
    template="plotly_white",
    color_discrete_sequence=["#f39c12"],
)
fig.update_layout(
    title=dict(text="Tasa de desempleo total en Chile (%)", font=dict(size=16)),
    font_family="Inter",
    xaxis=eje_x,
    yaxis=dict(tickformat=".1f"),
    margin=dict(b=120),
    height=450,
)
agregar_autor(fig)
fig.show()
```

## 2. Desempleo por sexo

```{python}
fig = go.Figure()
fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(
    x=dt["fecha"], y=dt["total"],
    name="Total", line=dict(color="#f39c12", width=2),
))
fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(
    x=dt["fecha"], y=dt["hombre"],
    name="Hombre", line=dict(color="#2980b9", width=2),
))
fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(
    x=dt["fecha"], y=dt["mujer"],
    name="Mujer", line=dict(color="#e74c3c", width=2),
))
fig.update_layout(
    title=dict(text="Tasa de desempleo por sexo (%)", font=dict(size=16)),
    yaxis_title="Tasa de desempleo (%)",
    xaxis=eje_x,
    template="plotly_white",
    font_family="Inter",
    legend=dict(orientation="h", y=-0.3, x=0.5, xanchor="center"),
    yaxis=dict(tickformat=".1f"),
    margin=dict(b=130),
    height=450,
)
agregar_autor(fig)
fig.show()
```

## 3. Brecha de desempleo mujer − hombre

```{python}
fig = px.area(
    dt, x="fecha", y="brecha",
    labels={"brecha": "Brecha (pp)", "fecha": ""},
    template="plotly_white",
    color_discrete_sequence=["#8e44ad"],
)
fig.add_hline(y=0, line_dash="dash", line_color="gray", opacity=0.5)
fig.update_layout(
    title=dict(text="Brecha de desempleo mujer − hombre (puntos porcentuales)", font=dict(size=16)),
    font_family="Inter",
    xaxis=eje_x,
    yaxis=dict(tickformat=".1f"),
    margin=dict(b=120),
    height=450,
)
agregar_autor(fig)
fig.show()
```

## 4. Duración promedio del desempleo

```{python}
#| echo: false
from IPython.display import Image
Image("../imagenes/trabajo/linkedin.png", width=800)
```
